ton-chi-muc-dich.content1: The Vietnam Women's Academy Scientific Journal publishes scientific research works in the fields of teaching and research conducted by staff, lecturers, and researchers both within and outside the Academy.
ton-chi-muc-dich.content2: Provides information on research results from domestic and international studies related to gender, women, and development to support the Academy's training and scientific research activities.
ton-chi-muc-dich.content3: Disseminates the policies, guidelines, and laws of the Party and the State in the fields of education and training, science and technology, as well as the scientific and technological activities of the Vietnam Women's Union.
ton-chi-muc-dich.content4: On January 24, 2018, the Vietnam Women's Academy Scientific Journal was established under Decision No. 01/QĐ-HVPNVN by the Director of the Vietnam Women's Academy and operates under Publishing License No. 561/GP-BTTTT issued on November 13, 2017.
ton-chi-muc-dich.content5: The Vietnam Women's Academy Scientific Journal has been recognized for scoring from 2020 onwards under Decision No. 18/QĐ-HĐGSNN dated June 30, 2020, issued by the State Professorship Council.
ton-chi-muc-dich.content6: The Vietnam Women's Academy Scientific Journal publishes articles in both Vietnamese and English. The journal has the ISSN number 2615-9007, is published quarterly, and has an electronic website at http://tapchikhoahoc.hvpnvn.edu.vn. On July 6, 2022, the Vietnam Women's Academy Scientific Journal was approved by the State Professorship Council to receive scientific scoring in two inter-disciplinary councils: Philosophy - Political Science - Sociology (0.5 points) and Economics (0.25 points), effective from 2022.

quy-trinh-phan-bien.content: The process for receiving and reviewing articles for the Vietnam Women's Academy Scientific Journal
quy-trinh-phan-bien.buoc1-1: Step 1. Announcement and invitation to submit articles for the Journal
quy-trinh-phan-bien.buoc1-2: Implementing the plan for publishing the Scientific Journal on a quarterly basis, the Secretary of the Editorial Office (TKTS) sends announcements and invitations to submit articles to organizations and individuals via email twice a month; posts on the official website of the Vietnam Women's Academy, the Journal's Facebook page, etc., including the guidelines for content and format requirements.
quy-trinh-phan-bien.buoc2-1: Step 2. Authors submit their articles to the Scientific Journal
quy-trinh-phan-bien.buoc2-2: Authors submit hard copies directly at the Journal's Editorial Office (Women's Research Institute, Room 410, Building A2, Vietnam Women's Academy), via email at tapchikh@vwa.edu.vn, or by logging into their account and submitting the article on the website: http://tapchikhoahoc.hvpnvn.edu.vn/. After completing all the steps in the submission process, the corresponding author of the article will receive a confirmation email that the submission process is complete.
quy-trinh-phan-bien.buoc3-1: Step 3. TKTS receives and conducts preliminary reviews of submitted articles
quy-trinh-phan-bien.buoc3-2: After receiving the submitted articles, TKTS conducts a preliminary review of both the format and content of the articles:
quy-trinh-phan-bien.buoc3-3: + If compliant: Accept the manuscript, communicate with the author, and send an email notification. Manuscripts that do not comply with the submission guidelines will be returned for the author to revise and complete.
quy-trinh-phan-bien.buoc3-4: + If non-compliant: The Journal decides to reject the article for not meeting the submission requirements, sending an email notification to the author (including reasons for rejection).
quy-trinh-phan-bien.buoc3-5: Timeline: 1-7 days after receiving the article
quy-trinh-phan-bien.buoc3-6: Note: At this step, revised articles (compliant with submission guidelines) must be resubmitted to the Editorial Office within 3 months from the date of receiving the revision request letter, unless approved by the Editor-in-Chief. Timeline: Throughout the year
quy-trinh-phan-bien.buoc4-1: Step 4. TKTS prepares a list of proposed reviewers for approval by the Editor-in-Chief (TBT)
quy-trinh-phan-bien.buoc4-2: After receiving articles that comply with the submission guidelines or have been revised per preliminary review feedback, TKTS prepares a list of proposed reviewers for each article and submits it to the Editor-in-Chief for approval. Reviewers are members of the Editorial Board.
quy-trinh-phan-bien.buoc4-3: Timeline: 1-7 days after receiving the articles for the upcoming issue of the Journal
quy-trinh-phan-bien.buoc5-1: Step 5. TKTS assigns reviewers from the approved list
quy-trinh-phan-bien.buoc5-2: Timeline: 1-3 days after receiving the proposed list from TKTS
quy-trinh-phan-bien.buoc6-1: Step 6. Reviewers conduct the peer-review process as assigned (Reviewers are members of the Editorial Board)
quy-trinh-phan-bien.buoc6-2: After the Editor-in-Chief approves the reviewer list, TKTS sends the articles along with review forms to the assigned reviewers. Reviewers are members of the Editorial Board as per the assigned list and will conduct the entire peer-review process (double-blind review) and monitor the author’s revisions related to the assigned articles.
quy-trinh-phan-bien.buoc6-3: TKTS serves as the contact point between the author and the assigned reviewer.
quy-trinh-phan-bien.buoc6-4: Editorial Board members act as reviewers, evaluating the article manuscripts based on set standards and timelines. They provide feedback on the review form regarding the quality of the articles with one of the following options:
quy-trinh-phan-bien.buoc6-5: a) The article is accepted for publication without any revisions;
quy-trinh-phan-bien.buoc6-6: b) The article is accepted for publication provided minor corrections or additions are made (mainly typos or phrasing issues);
quy-trinh-phan-bien.buoc6-7: c) The article requires major revisions and must be resubmitted for a second round of review;
quy-trinh-phan-bien.buoc6-8: d) The article is not accepted for publication.
quy-trinh-phan-bien.buoc6-9: Timeline: 1-7 days from the date TKTS sends the article to the reviewer
quy-trinh-phan-bien.buoc7-1: Step 7. Editor-in-Chief evaluates and categorizes the articles
quy-trinh-phan-bien.buoc7-2: Based on the feedback from reviewers and Deputy Editor-in-Chief, the Editor-in-Chief makes a decision in line with the options in the above steps, corresponding to cases T1, T2, T3, and T4 in the flowchart. The Editor-in-Chief's decision does not necessarily align with the reviewers' feedback.
quy-trinh-phan-bien.buoc7-3: * Case (T1): The article is accepted for publication without any revisions and is approved for publication in the Journal.
quy-trinh-phan-bien.buoc7-4: * Case (T2): The article is accepted for publication with minor corrections or additions per reviewers’ feedback (mainly typos or phrasing issues); the author revises and resubmits it to the Journal. TKTS reviews or directly corrects these for the author without resubmitting to the Editor-in-Chief.
quy-trinh-phan-bien.buoc7-5: * Case (T3): The article requires major revisions. Based on the reviewers' feedback, the author revises and resubmits the article to the Journal. The review process is conducted for a second time.
quy-trinh-phan-bien.buoc7-6: * Case (T4): The article is not accepted for publication in the Journal. TKTS sends an email rejecting the article to the author, specifying the feedback and reasons for rejection.
quy-trinh-phan-bien.buoc7-7: Timeline: 1-7 days from the date the reviewer sends their feedback, comments, and review form
quy-trinh-phan-bien.buoc8-1: Step 8. Authors revise and supplement their articles as required
quy-trinh-phan-bien.buoc8-2: For cases (T1) and (T2): Authors revise and supplement their articles as required, clearly indicating the revised content and reasons for retaining any unrevised content, if applicable.
quy-trinh-phan-bien.buoc8-3: Timeline: Within 3-5 days from the date the authors receive the reviewer’s feedback.
quy-trinh-phan-bien.buoc9-1: Step 9. Editor-in-Chief approves the finalized articles for publication
quy-trinh-phan-bien.buoc9-2: The Editor-in-Chief approves the articles for publication based on the Editorial Board's recommendations summarized by TKTS.
quy-trinh-phan-bien.buoc9-3: Timeline: 1-7 days from the date TKTS submits the summary list
quy-trinh-phan-bien.buoc10-1: Step 10. TKTS sends confirmation to the authors about their articles’ content and the expected publication date

quy-dinh-huong-dan.content: Regulations and Guidelines for Authors Submitting Articles
quy-dinh-huong-dan.hinh-thuc-2: - Articles should be formatted on A4 paper in portrait orientation, with a maximum length of 6,000 words excluding the abstract.
quy-dinh-huong-dan.hinh-thuc-3: - Encoding: Unicode; Font: Times New Roman; Font size: 13; Line spacing: single.
quy-dinh-huong-dan.hinh-thuc-4: - Normal character density, without compressing or stretching the spacing between characters.
quy-dinh-huong-dan.cau-truc-bai-bao-4: Abstract in Vietnamese: no more than 250 words, summarizing the most important points of the article. Although concise, it must include comprehensive content, including objectives, research scope, description of methods used, and research results. Everything mentioned in the abstract must appear in the main body.
quy-dinh-huong-dan.cau-truc-bai-bao-5: Abstract in English: translated from the Vietnamese abstract.
quy-dinh-huong-dan.tu-khoa: 1.3. Keywords (in Vietnamese and English): List 3-5 keywords.
quy-dinh-huong-dan.dat-van-de-1: Authors can start the article in various ways, but the following main points must be addressed: the urgency, significance, and impact of the research. The introduction should briefly present the current state of knowledge related to the problem introduced in the submitted article and how the problem has been approached.
quy-dinh-huong-dan.doi-tuong-va-phuong-phap-nghien-cuu-1: Clearly present the research objects and methods used in the study.
quy-dinh-huong-dan.ket-qua-nghien-cuu-1: Present only the results directly derived from the submitted study. Only these results are the subject of analysis and discussion in the article. In this section, authors provide their observations by comparing the study's results with previously published results in the same field. Emerging new directions should also be discussed.
quy-dinh-huong-dan.ket-luan-1: Reaffirm the findings derived from the study and present proposals or recommendations to relevant parties.
quy-dinh-huong-dan.tai-lieu-tham-khao-1: References and citations should follow the 6th edition of the APA style.
quy-dinh-huong-dan.tai-lieu-tham-khao-2: - In-text citations should be placed in parentheses, with the author's name, year, and page number (if available). Example: (Nguyen Du, 2006); (Trosborg, 1987, pp. 147-167).
quy-dinh-huong-dan.tai-lieu-tham-khao-3: - The article must include a reference section at the end, arranged alphabetically by the authors' last names. All citations in the article must appear in the reference list and vice versa.
quy-dinh-huong-dan.tai-lieu-tham-khao-4: - For books: Author's name (year of publication). Title (italicized). Publisher, location.
quy-dinh-huong-dan.tai-lieu-tham-khao-5: Example: i) Nguyen Du (2006). Khoi Lai Dong Xua. Lao Dong Publishing House, Hanoi.
quy-dinh-huong-dan.tai-lieu-tham-khao-6: ii) Porter, M. E. (1990). The Competitive Advantages of Nations. Free Press, New York.
quy-dinh-huong-dan.tai-lieu-tham-khao-7: - For journal articles: Author's name (year of publication). Article title (plain text). Journal Title (italicized), Volume (Issue), pages, DOI (if available).
quy-dinh-huong-dan.tai-lieu-tham-khao-8: Example: i) Ha Huu Nga (2015). Urban and Regional Economy. Sustainable Development Journal, 5(3), pp. 67-74.
quy-dinh-huong-dan.tai-lieu-tham-khao-9: ii) Trosborg, A. (1987). Apology strategies in natives/non-natives. Journal of Pragmatics, 11(1), pp. 147-167, doi:10.1086/245343.
quy-dinh-huong-dan.the-loai-tham-khao: 2. Reference and Opinion Articles Include the Following Sections:
quy-dinh-huong-dan.the-loai-khac: 3. Other Categories (translation, news, events, promotional information, etc.):
quy-dinh-huong-dan.the-loai-khac-1: Presented in accordance with the Press Law. - At the end of the article, include author information: Full name, academic rank, academic title, current position, workplace, contact address, phone number, and email. - For unused articles, the Journal does not return the manuscript.
quy-dinh-huong-dan.check-dao-van: 4. All articles submitted to the Journal will be checked for plagiarism using Simidoc software (accepted articles must have a duplication rate below 20%).
quy-dinh-huong-dan.gui-nhan-bai-2: Authors should submit their articles to the Journal by logging in and submitting on the website: http://tapchikhoahoc.hvpnvn.edu.vn or sending it to the email address: tapchikh@vwa.edu.vn.
quy-dinh-huong-dan.dia-chi-lien-he-2: Room 1503, 15th Floor, Building A2, Vietnam Women's Academy
quy-dinh-huong-dan.dia-chi-lien-he-3: Address: No. 68 Nguyen Chi Thanh Street, Dong Da District, Hanoi
quy-dinh-huong-dan.dia-chi-lien-he-5: Subscribe to the Journal at: https://forms.gle/qXrZhF33MPiDM47L7

chinh-sach-rut-bai.content1: - Regulations on canceling or withdrawing articles (applicable only to articles that have not been published in print/electronic formats) in the Vietnam Women's Academy Science Journal.
chinh-sach-rut-bai.content2: - Article cancellation is applied by the Editor-in-Chief for articles in the publishing process that are found to have:
chinh-sach-rut-bai.content3: Serious errors that alter the article's results;
chinh-sach-rut-bai.content4: Content that overlaps with another article already published;
chinh-sach-rut-bai.content5: Authors violating ethical research and scientific publication practices such as submitting articles to multiple journals simultaneously, falsifying authorship declarations, plagiarism, fabricating data, and similar cases;
chinh-sach-rut-bai.content6: - Authors are allowed to withdraw their articles during the first review round. After the second review round, authors cannot withdraw their articles without the Editor-in-Chief's approval. Withdrawal of published articles is done based on the request of:
chinh-sach-rut-bai.content7: The author and/or co-authors of the article; or
chinh-sach-rut-bai.content8: The Editor-in-Chief upon the recommendation of members of the scientific community.

huong-dan-gui-bai.title: Submission Instructions
huong-dan-gui-bai.content: Instructions for submitting articles directly on the website (for authors)
huong-dan-gui-bai.dang-ky-b2: Step 2: In the top right corner of the website, click on 'Register' or 'Đăng ký'.
huong-dan-gui-bai.dang-ky-b3: Step 3: Enter all required information (fields marked with * are mandatory), then click 'Register Account'.
huong-dan-gui-bai.dang-ky-b4: Step 4: After creating the account, the system will send login details to the email entered above. Click on the link to log in to the system.
huong-dan-gui-bai.dang-ky-b5: Step 5: Click the link to access the software, then click 'log in' to log in. After logging in, you need to change the password. (Remember the new password for future access).
huong-dan-gui-bai.dang-nhap-b2: Step 2: In the top right corner of the website, click on 'Login' or 'Đăng nhập'.
huong-dan-gui-bai.dang-nhap-b3: Step 3: Enter the email and password you just registered with.
